title: bupleurum falcatum var. pusillum
intro: Bupleurum falcatum var. pusillum, also known as Chinese kuzu or small-leaved hare's ear, is a variety of the Bupleurum falcatum plant, a flowering plant in the carrot family. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and lowering blood pressure, due to its ability to clear heat and toxins from the body. In herbal preparations, Bupleurum falcatum var. pusillum is often combined with other herbs to make teas, capsules, or tinctures, which are used to support liver health and immune function.
